name: getModule
description: Returns a pointer to a registered StelModule by its ID (which is the module's class name by convention). If the module is not found, a warning is logged unless noWarning is set to true. This is the primary method for accessing any module in Stellarium from scripts. The convenience macro GETSTELMODULE(ModuleName) is the recommended way to call this.

parameters.name: moduleID
parameters.description: The module's unique ID, by convention equal to the class name (e.g., 'ConstellationMgr', 'MilkyWay', 'LandscapeMgr')

parameters.name: noWarning
parameters.description: If true, suppresses the warning message when the module is not found (useful for optional plugins)

returnDescription: Pointer to the module instance, or null if not found

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions. The macro GETSTELMODULE(ModuleName) is the standard way to call this.

name: getAllModules
description: Returns a list of all currently registered StelModule instances, including both core modules and loaded plugins. Use this to enumerate all available modules and their capabilities at runtime.
returnDescription: List of all registered module pointers

name: getCallOrders
description: Returns the ordered list of modules that will be called for a specific action type. Actions include drawing, updating, handling mouse clicks, mouse moves, and key presses. The order is determined by each module's getCallOrder() method.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action type to query. Possible values: ActionDraw, ActionUpdate, ActionHandleMouseClicks, ActionHandleMouseMoves, ActionHandleKeys

returnDescription: Ordered list of module pointers for the specified action

compatibility.notes: Primarily useful for debugging module execution order

name: loadPlugin
description: Dynamically loads a plugin by its module ID. The plugin must be installed in the modules directory. Returns the loaded StelModule instance which still needs to be initialized. If the plugin is not found, a warning is logged and null is returned.

parameters.name: moduleID
parameters.description: The plugin's unique ID (matches the directory name and shared library name, e.g., 'Oculars', 'Satellites')

returnDescription: Pointer to the loaded module, or null if loading failed

compatibility.notes: The returned module still needs initialization

name: unloadAllPlugins
description: Unloads all currently loaded plugins from memory. This calls unloadModule() for each loaded plugin and updates the calling lists to remove references to deleted plugin modules. Useful for cleaning up before loading a different set of plugins.

compatibility.notes: Unloads plugins in reverse load order to handle dependencies properly

name: setPluginLoadAtStartup
description: Sets whether a plugin should be automatically loaded when Stellarium starts. The setting is saved to the application configuration file, so it persists across sessions. The key is the plugin's unique ID.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The plugin's unique ID (same as moduleID, e.g., 'Oculars', 'Satellites')

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to enable loading at startup, false to disable

compatibility.notes: Setting is saved to QSettings and persists across sessions

name: isPluginLoaded
description: Checks whether a specific plugin is currently loaded in memory. Returns true if the plugin has been loaded via loadPlugin() or was auto-loaded at startup.
returnDescription: true if the plugin is loaded, false otherwise

name: getPluginsList
description: Returns a list of all available plugins (both static and dynamic) found in the modules directories. Each PluginDescriptor contains information about the plugin (id, name, description, version) and its current state (loadAtStartup, loaded). This includes both loaded and unloaded plugins.
returnDescription: List of all available plugin descriptors with metadata and state

compatibility.notes: Scans modules/ directory for dynamic plugins and includes static plugins

name: getExtensionList
description: Returns a list of all currently registered extensions. Extensions are QObject instances provided by plugins that offer additional functionality beyond the main StelModule interface. If using this method, you should also listen to the extensionsAdded() signal to handle all possible initialization orders.
returnDescription: List of all registered extension objects

compatibility.notes: Extensions are provided by plugins through StelPluginInterface::getExtensionList()

name: loadExtensions
description: Loads all extensions provided by a specific plugin module. Extensions are additional QObject instances that plugins can provide beyond their main StelModule class. After loading, the extensions are added to the extension list and the extensionsAdded signal is emitted.

parameters.name: moduleID
parameters.description: The plugin's unique ID to load extensions from

returnDescription: List of loaded extension objects, or empty list if plugin not found

name: getStandardSupportLinksInfo
description: Returns a standardized HTML string containing support information and links for a module. Includes links to the Stellarium support forum, GitHub issues page, and optionally the developer documentation. The module name is used in the instructional text to help users identify which module they should mention in support requests.

parameters.name: moduleName
parameters.description: The module name to include in the support information text

parameters.name: furtherInfo
parameters.description: If true, includes a link to the developer documentation

returnDescription: HTML formatted string with support links and information

compatibility.notes: Returns HTML string suitable for display in a QTextBrowser or similar widget
